output:
CVE-2021-4170 identifies a Cross-Site Scripting (XSS) vulnerability in calibre-web, a web-based application for managing e-book libraries. This vulnerability exists due to improper neutralization of user input during web page generation (CWE-79). Attackers can exploit this flaw by injecting malicious scripts into web pages viewed by other users, potentially leading to unauthorized actions or data theft. 

The severity of this vulnerability is rated as medium, indicating that it poses a significant risk, but may require certain conditions to be met to exploit. Versions of calibre-web prior to 0.6.15 are affected by this vulnerability. It is crucial for users to update to the latest version to mitigate this issue. 

The associated MITRE ATT&CK technique is T1190, which relates to exploitation for credential access and exfiltration. Mitigation strategies include input validation (M1016) and implementing security controls for web content (M1048), along with regular updates and security patches.
